summarization.a: The plot shows etch depth versus number of ALE cycles for five materials. Al₂O₃, HfO₂, AlNₓ, and SiO₂ exhibit linear increases in etch depth with cycles. TiN shows essentially no etching, remaining near zero throughout. SiO₂ has the highest etch rate while TiN acts as an etch stop.

a.question: List the materials plotted in the figure, together with the marker used for each.
a.answer:
Al₂O₃ – black squares

HfO₂ – red circles

AlNₓ – blue triangles

SiO₂ – black asterisks

TiN – red crosses

a.question: What activation-step duration was used to generate all the curves in Fig. 6?
How much Al₂O₃ will have been removed after 40 cycles?
What is the practical implication of all slopes being stable up to ~40 cycles?
a.answer: The process doesn't drift quickly with time. That allows predictable depth control just by choosing the cycle number.
